skill.name: commit
skill.description: Create Sentry-style commit messages with conventional commit types, clear subjects, useful bodies, and issue references.
skill.summary: Draft commit messages that follow Sentry conventions and link related issues.
skill.author: getsentry

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command and weak-crypto patterns, but review found they are markdown examples and commit-message templates, not executable skill code. The only network indicator is a documentation link to Sentry engineering practices, with no evidence of data collection, credential access, prompt injection, or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: External Command Examples Are Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The reported shell-command matches appear inside markdown examples for checking a git branch, creating a feature branch, and formatting commit messages. This is expected for a commit-message skill, but users should still review git commands before execution because branch commands can change local repository state.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched content is fenced markdown and commit-message formatting text. Only the git branch and checkout examples are operational commands, and they align with the stated purpose of the skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography findings at the reported lines do not show cryptographic algorithms, hashing APIs, encoded payloads, or security-sensitive transformations. They appear to be triggered by ordinary commit-message and branch-naming text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no MD5, SHA1, cipher, signature, or password-handling logic at these locations. The lines describe the skill purpose and branch naming examples.

low_findings.title: Documentation Link Does Not Exfiltrate Data
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is a reference link to Sentry commit-message documentation. There is no instruction to fetch the URL automatically, transmit local data, or send credentials to an external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network indicator is a plain markdown link in a references section. No surrounding content instructs automated network access or data upload.

content.value_statement: Inconsistent commit messages make review and release history harder to scan. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to produce Sentry-style conventional commits with useful context and issue references.
actual_capabilities: Formats commit messages with type, optional scope, subject, body, and footer.
Selects Sentry commit types such as feat, fix, ref, docs, test, build, and ci.
Keeps subject lines concise and uses imperative present-tense wording.
Adds issue footers using Fixes or Refs patterns for GitHub, Sentry, or Linear identifiers.
Provides examples for fixes, features, refactors, breaking changes, and reverts.
limitations: Does not inspect git history unless the assistant separately runs git commands.
Does not validate that issue IDs exist in GitHub, Sentry, or Linear.
Does not replace project-specific release or compliance review.
Assumes Sentry conventions are appropriate for the target repository.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Pull Request Commit
use_cases.description: Turn a completed code change into a concise commit message with the right type, scope, and issue footer.

use_cases.title: Normalize Team Commit History
use_cases.description: Rewrite unclear draft messages into consistent Sentry-style conventional commits before review or merge.

use_cases.title: Document Bug Fix Motivation
use_cases.description: Add a clear body that explains the previous behavior, the new behavior, and the linked issue.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Commit Message
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for one straightforward change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a Sentry-style commit message for this change: [describe the change]. Use the best commit type and keep the subject under 70 characters.

prompt_templates.title: Add Scope and Issue Reference
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt when the component and issue are known.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a commit message for [change summary]. Use scope [component] and include [Fixes or Refs] [issue ID] in the footer.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Draft Message
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for editing an existing message.
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this draft to follow Sentry commit conventions. Keep the intent, improve the subject and body, and preserve the issue reference: [draft message].

prompt_templates.title: Split Complex Work Into Commits
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for a multi-part change set.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these changes and propose separate Sentry-style commit messages. Each message must represent one stable, reviewable change: [change list].

output_examples.input: A dashboard fix prevents deleted users from causing a null response crash, linked to SENTRY-5678.
output_examples.output:
fix(dashboard): Handle deleted user responses

Deleted users could return empty response data and crash the dashboard. Add a guard before reading user fields.

Fixes SENTRY-5678

output_examples.input: An alerts feature posts updates into an existing Slack thread and references GH-1234.
output_examples.output:
feat(alerts): Add Slack thread replies for alert updates

Alert updates now reply to the original Slack thread instead of creating a new message. This keeps related notifications grouped together.

Refs GH-1234

output_examples.input: A refactor moves repeated validation from three endpoints into one shared validator.
output_examples.output:
ref: Extract shared endpoint validation

Move duplicate validation from three endpoints into a shared validator. No behavior change.

best_practices: Describe one stable change per commit message.
Use the body to explain what changed and why it changed.
Use Fixes only when the commit should close the referenced issue.
anti_patterns: Do not combine unrelated feature, refactor, and formatting changes in one commit.
Do not use vague subjects such as update code or fix bug.
Do not include sensitive internal details in public commit messages.

faq.question: Does this skill create git commits automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides commit-message guidance and examples. The assistant may run git commands only if the user asks.

faq.question: Which commit format does it use?
faq.answer: It uses Sentry conventions based on conventional commits, with type, optional scope, subject, body, and footer.

faq.question: Can it include issue references?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports Fixes and Refs footers for GitHub, Sentry, and Linear-style issue identifiers.

faq.question: Can it handle breaking changes?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows the breaking-change marker in the header and a BREAKING CHANGE footer.

faq.question: Does it verify issue IDs?
faq.answer: No. It formats issue references but does not confirm that the linked issue exists.

faq.question: Is it useful outside Sentry repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, if the project accepts similar conventional commit rules. Teams should adjust wording to local policy.
